Scenario.title: SPACEHOP Cyber Attack on Transportation Systems

overview.description: The SPACEHOP Activity targets a transportation system operator using a multi-stage cyber attack to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data. The attack leverages spear phishing, malware deployment, and privilege escalation to achieve its objectives.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt global supply chains and gather strategic intelligence

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 163 Name: Spear Phishing Description: Targets specific users with tailored phishing attacks to gain initial access.
stage.description: An email phishing campaign impersonating a trusted vendor tricks employees into clicking a malicious link, exploiting CVE-2021-26086 to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 445 Name: Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation Description: Exploits configuration management systems to insert malicious logic into software builds.
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers deploy custom malware targeting known vulnerabilities in the transportation system's software, leveraging CVE-2012-0754 to establish a foothold.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 645 Name: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket) Description: Uses stolen Kerberos tickets to maintain access despite system reboots.
stage.description: Adversaries create a new user account with elevated privileges, ensuring persistent access to the network despite potential system reboots or updates.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 8 Name: Buffer Overflow in an API Call Description: Exploits buffer overflow vulnerabilities in shared libraries to gain control over system functions.
stage.description: Known or zero-day vulnerabilities in the transportation system software are exploited to escalate privileges and gain full control over critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 216 Name: Communication Channel Manipulation Description: Encrypts malicious traffic to avoid detection by network monitoring tools.
stage.description: Communication channels are encrypted to evade detection by network monitoring tools, ensuring the attack remains undetected while establishing persistence.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 440 Name: Hardware Integrity Attack Description: Executes commands to disrupt operational systems, causing downtime and safety risks.
stage.description: Commands are executed to disrupt traffic management systems, leading to significant operational downtime and safety risks for the transportation network.

Scenario.summary: The SPACEHOP Activity successfully compromises a transportation system through a multi-stage attack involving spear phishing, malware deployment, and privilege escalation. By encrypting communication channels and maintaining persistence, the adversaries ensure long-term access to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data.
